title: Ribbit: Why Jews Cannot Charge Interest to Other Jews
title_zh: 利息禁令（Ribbit）：为什么犹太人不能向同胞收取利息
title_he: רִבִּית: מדוע אסור ליהודים לגבות ריבית מיהודים אחרים
key_concepts: Biblical basis in Exodus, Leviticus, Deuteronomy
historical_period: Given at Mount Sinai (~1300 BCE, Late Shang Dynasty 商朝)
cultural_parallels: Confucian emphasis on 仁 (benevolence) in economic relations
义利之辨 (righteousness vs. profit debate) in Chinese philosophy
Gemach system as communal mutual aid
practical_applications: Use of heter iska for mortgages and business loans
Credit card considerations with Jewish-owned banks
Personal lending between friends and family
target_audience: Mandarin-speaking readers with no prior Jewish knowledge
author: Torah Light Project
